what_it_does: This JSON file is an interpretive ledger entry for the source file app/ai/ai-seed.js. It records a model-generated, file-local analysis: a factual summary of what the source file does, notable observations, standout patterns, conventions, concerns, open questions, purpose-vs-actual assessment, apparent themes, inferred intention, novelty, contribution, and two embedding-oriented summaries. It carries no code symbols.
observations: The entry is model-attributed to 'opus' and maps to source path 'app/ai/ai-seed.js'.
The 'what_it_does' field states the source file is an IIFE that seeds the CV_AI registry with helper-free built-in providers, behaviours, skills, context resolvers, and image capabilities.

Observations enumerate specific seeded items: three providers (claude, openai-image, vision), three behaviours (voice.conceptv, angle, diversity), eight skills (seven transform skills plus theme), four context resolvers (generic/pages/widget/wizard), and two image capabilities.
The entry notes loud load-order enforcement, the vision provider being exportOnly/unavailable in browser sandbox, and a documented fallback to local pixel analysis.
Standouts call out single-sourcing of the ConceptV voice, parameterisation of the angle behaviour, surface-keyed context resolvers with a generic floor, and honest runtime availability declaration.
Conventions listed include pure-data seeding, split seeding by dependency on composer helpers, silent registration, and a no-second-home rule.
The 'concerns' array is empty, indicating no file-local smells were identified by the authoring model.
Questions ask how dangling transform/theme skills are guaranteed a capability and how the vision fallback is wired given the registry's loud-fail stance.
The 'purpose_vs_actual' field asserts the source file's stated purpose matches its contents.
The 'symbols' array is absent of code symbols, consistent with the note '(No code symbols in this file — symbols[] = [].)'.
standouts: Dense, structured interpretive snapshot of a source file with separate factual, inferential, and embedding-oriented fields.
Explicit empty 'concerns' array and file-local questions make uncertainty machine-readable.
Includes both a one-line 'notes' field and longer 'what_it_does' for different consumption depths.
conventions: Follows a fixed interpretive schema with required top-level keys.

Includes model attribution and embedding summaries for downstream retrieval.
Keeps claims file-local and avoids cross-file ranking.

questions: What process validates that the interpretive claims remain accurate as the source file evolves?

purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file's purpose is to serve as a neutral interpretive ledger entry for app/ai/ai-seed.js, and that is exactly what its content does.
intention_signals: Trying to produce a machine-readable interpretive record of a source file so downstream fusion, search, or review can understand its responsibilities, design choices, and open questions without reading the code directly.
novelty: The interpretive layer combines factual description, inferred intention, and two distinct embedding summaries in one schema, while explicitly surfacing empty concerns and file-local questions.
contribution: Contributes a structured, queryable interpretation of app/ai/ai-seed.js, including its seeded responsibilities, design conventions, standout patterns, unresolved dependencies, and embedding-oriented summaries.
